source: Invest Like the Best — Tech and consumer growth investing (EP.149)
summary: Data quantity is the single most predictive variable of AI quality, creating compounding moats for dominant-scale internet platforms.

tickers_audited.reasoning: Baker explicitly cited Google Research papers as the empirical basis for the data-scaling law, and Google is the canonical dominant-scale internet platform he uses to illustrate the thesis. Directly connected.

While Meta is an obvious beneficiary of the data-quantity thesis, Baker did not specifically name Facebook/Meta in connection with this exact structural claim in the transcript. It appears to have been included by inference rather than explicit connection.

Amazon is a plausible beneficiary of the data-scaling thesis, but Baker did not specifically name Amazon in connection with this exact claim. Its inclusion appears inferential rather than based on Baker explicitly linking it to this thesis.

Baker explicitly cited Microsoft Research papers alongside Google Research as the empirical source of the data-scaling law. Microsoft is directly named as a contributor to the foundational research underpinning this thesis, making it a specific and explicit connection.
